pieces.label: A Vast City
pieces.note: Skyline of a vast city rendered in shaded block characters (▓██▓, ▒████▒, ▓███▓ buildings) framed by a ░ stippled border. Produced in response to the user's prompt 'show me a vast city'. The entire assistant message is this single ASCII composition inside a code fence. NOTE: the source transcript is itself truncated mid-piece (cut off partway through line ~17), so the deterministic extractor will only capture the bytes present in the source.

missed_or_ambiguous: Single assistant message (MSG 2) with exactly one piece of ASCII art (a city skyline), matching header's already_extracted_pieces: 1. The art is whole-message (begins at the opening code fence on line 12 and runs to end of message), so mode 'full' on MSG 2 is correct; no embedded prose to bound. Caveat: the transcript file is truncated mid-art — it ends abruptly with block characters and a trailing blank line, so the captured piece will be incomplete relative to whatever the model originally produced. No fragmentation across messages, no wrongly-merged pieces, no sequences.
